source.definition: A single-celled microorganism, often significant in health and disease.

explanation.sourceText: The English word "bacterium" comes from the Greek word "baktērion," meaning 'small staff or rod,' and entered English through Latin.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „bacterium” provine din grecescul „baktērion”, care înseamnă „băț mic”, și a fost preluat în engleză prin latină.

target.definition: Un microorganism unicelular, adesea relevant pentru sănătate sau boală.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word "bacterie" was borrowed from the French "bactérie," which in turn comes from the Greek "baktērion" (meaning 'small staff').
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „bacterie” a fost preluat din francezul „bactérie”, care provine din grecescul „baktērion” (însemnând „băț mic”).

examples.sourceText: You can see bacteria under the microscope.

learningTips.text: The Romanian word "bacterie" sounds very similar to the English "bacterium," making it easy to remember.
The ending -ie is common in Romanian nouns, especially scientific terms, similar to English words ending in -y or -ia.
Remember the Greek root "baktērion," meaning 'small rod,' which describes the shape of many bacteria.
Link "bacterie" with "antibiotic"—both are key terms in health and medicine.
